summary: Iteration 4 — CRM (status pipeline, follow-up date, tags, comments) and Social Connections (DB-stored credentials per platform) tested end-to-end. 17/17 new backend pytest cases pass (PATCH lead status/follow_up/tags, comments add+delete, social credentials GET/PUT/DELETE, validation errors, config merge with instagram→true after PUT, then back to false after DELETE). Frontend: login → /admin/leads → search input + 7 status pills + 25 lead rows + slide-over LeadDetailPanel render correctly. Verified live via DevTools Network: 2× PATCH /api/leads/{id} → 200 (follow-up + tag), 1× POST /api/leads/{id}/comments → 200, plus GET /api/social/credentials & /api/social/config (200) on Connections render. Connect modal for Instagram opens with both required fields and proper docs link. Status filter pill 'CONTACTED' updated count from 1→2 after status PATCH, confirming parent state lifted. No console errors during CRM/Connections flows.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking a status button (e.g. CONTACTED) auto-closes the slide-over panel before the user can also set follow-up/tag/comment in the same session. Behaviour may be intentional (single-purpose action) but UX-wise feels abrupt — consider keeping panel open and updating the active-status indicator inline.

action_items: (LOW) LeadDetailPanel.jsx — keep slide-over open after status PATCH so admin can chain status + follow-up + tag + comment in one session.

(NICE-TO-HAVE) AdminSocial.jsx — verified that platform pills with '· MOCK' are still present after a successful PUT credentials. Backend GET /api/social/config flips instagram→true correctly (verified in pytest), so the frontend just needs to refetch /api/social/config after a successful connect/disconnect to re-render the platform pills without that MOCK badge. Backend already supports it; only an effect/refetch is missing.

(VERY LOW) Connect modal: token field uses HTML5 'required' which is fine; consider adding data-testid="cred-{field}" to each input for deterministic test selectors (currently both inputs resolve via fuzzy placeholder match).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py update_lead: handles follow_up_date='' → None correctly. Uses model_dump(exclude_unset=True) so missing keys do not overwrite existing data. GOOD.
server.py upsert_credentials: rejects unknown platform with 400, missing required field with 400 (specific 'Missing field: X' detail). Cleaned values stripped. Atomic mongo upsert. GOOD.
server.py _mask: prefix3 + 8 dots + suffix3 — never returns the raw token. Verified that masked != raw in pytest. GOOD.
social_router /config: merges DB-configured AND env-configured per platform via OR — correct precedence. After PUT instagram with full fields, config[instagram]=True; after DELETE → False. Verified.

Lead model: status default 'new', follow_up_date Optional[str], tags List[str] default empty, comments List[dict] default empty. ConfigDict(extra='ignore') ensures legacy docs without these fields still hydrate. GOOD.

Lead PATCH: returns full updated lead doc (with _id stripped via {'_id': 0}); frontend can directly use it to update parent state — supports the 'badge updates in table after panel close' acceptance criterion.
POST /leads/{id}/comments returns ONLY the new comment object (not the whole lead) — frontend appends to local state. Trade-off acceptable but a refetch-on-add would also be safe.
PLATFORM_FIELDS dict is duplicated across server.py and social_publish.py module — minor DRY: consider single source of truth.
No console errors during all admin CRUD flows. CORS still configured with explicit origin + credentials. Auth cookies still HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=None.
Mock publishers still return ok:true,mocked:true — expected per request brief; LIVE Resend email + LIVE AI generation untouched.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_crm_and_connections.py (NEW — 17 tests covering CRM PATCH/comments + social credentials CRUD + config merge)

success_rate.frontend: ~95% — all acceptance criteria met; one LOW-priority panel-close UX item

test_credentials: admin@lumierebysambita.com / Lumiere@2026 (admin, seeded from /app/backend/.env). NOTE: previous iter3 test file uses sambita@lumierebysambita.com which still works (legacy seed). Use admin@ going forward — matches /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_CRM_<hex> leads via PUBLIC POST /api/leads (covered by fresh_lead_id fixture). All instagram/facebook/twitter/linkedin credentials are deleted in the same test class teardown. No persistent test data leaks except the disposable TEST_-prefixed leads.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three pytest suites now exist — /app/backend/tests/test_leads_api.py (8 tests, public lead create/list), /app/backend/tests/test_auth_and_social.py (18 tests, auth + drafts), /app/backend/tests/test_crm_and_connections.py (17 tests, CRM + connections). Run: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://lumiere-interior.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v`. Brute-force test still uses disposable email so admin lockout never happens. AI generate is invoked exactly once (session-scoped shared_draft_id) — no extra LLM credits burned by re-running. UI: lead-row-{uuid} testids, filter-{status} pill testids, connect-{platform} button testid, cred-save button testid all confirmed present.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no blocking issues. One LOW-priority UX nit: status-button click closes the slide-over panel; if user wants to also set follow-up/tags/comments they have to re-open the panel. Functionally correct (PATCH succeeds, badge updates) — pure UX polish.
